storage_policy.copyright_boundary: Store source identity, locator ranges, short beat observations, and derived presentation rules only. Do not store source video, frames, audio, full transcript text, screenshots, layouts, files, or brand marks.

beats.observed_demo_move: The demonstration opens with one wide visual field and enough attention space for the claim to land before proof density appears.
beats.derived_presentation_rule: The deck should open with a sparse native cover field, a single editable headline, and no explanatory panel row until the audience understands the arena.
reveal_sequence: native editable headline appears before support text
native proof marker stays absent until the next beat
beats.pacing_signal: Hold the first beat visually sparse; do not spend the opening on mechanism detail.
beats.qa_probe: From a static contact sheet, slide 1 should still read as an attention reset: one dominant field, low word count, and no proof grid.

beats.locator: course module transformation beat / 05:30-07:00 mode shift
beats.observed_demo_move: Teaching material is strongest when it visibly changes a weak input into a designed output instead of describing design quality in abstract terms.
beats.derived_presentation_rule: The contrast slide must use native before and after mini-previews, where the after state enlarges one proof object and deletes or routes excess labels.
reveal_sequence: native before thumbnail appears as a flat report state
native after thumbnail reveals the enlarged focal object
native delta label explains what changed
beats.pacing_signal: Use reveal order to make transformation legible before adding supporting evidence.
beats.qa_probe: The reviewer should identify the transformation direction without reading body text.

beats.observed_demo_move: Proof becomes easier to follow when each added layer has a clear entrance and the headline carries the meaning instead of a transcript-like paragraph.
beats.derived_presentation_rule: The proof slide should build from compressed native headline to proof object to small route marker, with evidence detail routed to trace or notes.
reveal_sequence: native headline states the proof claim
native proof object reveals after the claim
native route marker shows where detail was moved
beats.pacing_signal: Let the proof object expand while transcript-like explanation contracts.
beats.qa_probe: The proof slide should show a build path, not a paragraph pasted into a slide body.

beats.observed_demo_move: The strongest public demo moment breaks from setup density by making the outcome object visibly larger than previous proof surfaces.
beats.derived_presentation_rule: The climax slide must scale one native outcome object to dominate the canvas and reduce minor labels rather than repeat the prior proof grid.
reveal_sequence: native support labels fade into subordinate role
native outcome object scales up as the main visual
native comparison label locks the result
beats.pacing_signal: Increase object scale and reduce text at the climax.
beats.qa_probe: The climax should be visible as the largest object in the contact sheet.

beats.observed_demo_move: A public presentation close should simplify and hand the audience to a decision rather than add a final proof table.
beats.derived_presentation_rule: The close slide must reveal a native public-blocked gate and one decision handoff, with supporting detail kept outside the main visual field.
reveal_sequence: native public-blocked gate reveals as the decision object
native handoff line closes the sequence
beats.pacing_signal: Use visual relief after the climax; do not add new dense evidence.
beats.qa_probe: The close should look like a handoff gate, not an appendix or report status page.

qa_gates: each beat references known multimodal records, anchors, and video cards
each beat stores derived observation and presentation rule only
each beat maps to one motion role
raw media, copied frames, audio, full transcript text, and source files remain forbidden
public release remains blocked until render, provenance, and human approval
